title: When millions of AI agents meet
summary: Nenad Tomašev discusses the theoretical framework of an "agentic economy" where millions of AI agents autonomously execute plans, transact, negotiate, and delegate to one another. The conversation covers operational shifts to cooperative "societies of specialists," risks including automation bias and cybersecurity threats like dynamic cloaking and agentic traps, and the architectural challenges of distributed intelligence.
writeup: Nenad Tomašev lays out a theoretical framework for an "agentic economy" in which millions of AI agents autonomously plan, transact, negotiate, and delegate to one another. The discussion frames an operational shift toward cooperative "societies of specialists" and examines the architectural challenges of distributed intelligence and agent orchestration. It also catalogs the risks: automation bias, plus cybersecurity threats including dynamic cloaking and agentic traps. The relevance lies in treating multi-agent coordination, not single-model capability, as the unit of analysis for where agentic systems are headed.
